112.description: Data loaded from CSV file: 112.csv (Dataset: Caffeine Content of Drinks)

fields.name: drink
fields.sample_values: 28 Black Energy Drink, 3 Water, 3D Energy Drink, 4 Purpose Energy Drink, 4C Energy Drink Mix, ..., ZipFizz Energy Drink Mix, Zoa Energy Drink, Zola Coconut Water Espresso, Zombie Blood Energy Potion, doc Soda
fields.inferred_semantic_summary: This field identifies the specific brand and variety of beverage products—including energy drinks, sodas, water, and drink mixes—whose caffeine content is being measured or analyzed in the dataset.

fields.name: Volume (ml)
fields.inferred_semantic_summary: This field represents the measured liquid volume, in milliliters, of individual drink servings in the context of analyzing caffeine content across various beverage products.

fields.name: Calories
fields.inferred_semantic_summary: This field represents the number of calories contained in each drink, quantifying the energy provided per serving, which is relevant for nutrition and health tracking in beverages.

fields.name: Caffeine (mg)
fields.inferred_semantic_summary: This field represents the measured amount of caffeine, in milligrams, contained in various beverages, indicating their stimulant potency for dietary or health considerations.

fields.name: type
fields.sample_values: Coffee, Energy Drinks, Energy Shots, Soft Drinks, Tea, Water
fields.inferred_semantic_summary: This field categorizes beverages by their general drink type, indicating the classification of each entry (e.g., coffee, tea, water) within the context of analyzing caffeine content.

12.description: Data loaded from CSV file: 12.csv (Dataset: Career Prediction Dataset)

fields.name: O_score
fields.inferred_semantic_summary: O_score represents a quantitative assessment of an individual's openness to experience, likely derived from a personality test, used in career prediction to gauge trait levels between 2.67 and 9.45.

fields.name: C_score
fields.inferred_semantic_summary: C_score likely represents a standardized numerical assessment (such as a composite or cumulative score) quantifying a candidate's career-related performance or potential within an academic or professional context.

fields.name: E_score
fields.inferred_semantic_summary: E_score likely represents a quantified measure of a candidate's aptitude or proficiency in a specific subject or skill area (such as English), used in career or academic prediction contexts, with scores ranging from 2.89 to 9.34.

fields.name: A_score
fields.inferred_semantic_summary: A_score likely represents a numerical evaluation (such as an academic score, aptitude test score, or performance rating) used to assess an individual's suitability or qualification for a particular career in the context of career prediction.

fields.name: N_score
fields.inferred_semantic_summary: N_score likely represents a numerical assessment of a personality trait, such as Neuroticism, measured on a standardized scale for individuals in the context of predicting career suitability or outcomes.

fields.name: Numerical Aptitude
fields.inferred_semantic_summary: Numerical Aptitude measures an individual's quantitative reasoning or mathematical skill level, likely scored through an assessment, to predict suitability for various careers requiring mathematical competence.

fields.name: Spatial Aptitude
fields.inferred_semantic_summary: Spatial Aptitude quantifies an individual's ability to understand and visualize spatial relationships, measured on a numerical scale and used to predict suitability for careers requiring spatial reasoning skills.

fields.name: Perceptual Aptitude
fields.inferred_semantic_summary: Perceptual Aptitude quantifies an individual's ability to accurately interpret and respond to sensory information, measured on a scale likely used for career suitability or cognitive assessment.

fields.name: Abstract Reasoning
fields.inferred_semantic_summary: This field quantifies individuals' scores on abstract reasoning assessments (likely cognitive ability tests), reflecting their problem-solving and logical thinking skills relevant to career suitability and prediction.

fields.name: Verbal Reasoning
fields.inferred_semantic_summary: The "Verbal Reasoning" field quantifies an individual's ability to understand and analyze written information, likely as a standardized test score used in career aptitude assessment.

fields.name: Career
fields.sample_values: Accountant, Administrative Officer, Advertising Executive, Aerospace Engineer, Air Traffic Controller, ..., Video Game Tester, Web Developer, Wildlife Biologist, Wildlife Conservationist, Zoologist
fields.inferred_semantic_summary: This field represents the specific job titles or professions individuals pursue or aspire to within various industries, serving as a categorical indicator of career choice in the context of occupational prediction and guidance.

122.description: Data loaded from CSV file: 122.csv (Dataset: Video Game Sales)

fields.name: Rank
fields.inferred_semantic_summary: The "Rank" field assigns a unique sequential identifier to each video game in the sales dataset, indicating its position or order within the entire list of recorded games.

fields.name: Name
fields.sample_values: '98 Koshien, .hack//G.U. Vol.1//Rebirth, .hack//G.U. Vol.2//Reminisce, .hack//G.U. Vol.2//Reminisce (jp sales), .hack//G.U. Vol.3//Redemption, ..., thinkSMART: Chess for Kids, uDraw Studio, uDraw Studio: Instant Artist, wwe Smackdown vs. Raw 2006, ¡Shin Chan Flipa en colores!
fields.inferred_semantic_summary: The "Name" field identifies the official titles of individual video games, serving as unique product identifiers within the video game sales dataset.

fields.name: Platform
fields.sample_values: 2600, 3DO, 3DS, DC, DS, ..., Wii, WiiU, X360, XB, XOne
fields.inferred_semantic_summary: The "Platform" field indicates the specific gaming console or system on which a video game was released, identifying the hardware used to play the game in the context of tracking sales across different video game platforms.

fields.name: Year
fields.sample_values: nan, nan, nan, nan, nan, ..., nan, nan, nan, nan, nan
fields.inferred_semantic_summary: This field represents the release year (from 1980 to 2020) in which a video game became available, indicating the time frame of each game's launch within the video game sales industry.

fields.name: Genre
fields.sample_values: Action, Adventure, Fighting, Misc, Platform, ..., Role-Playing, Shooter, Simulation, Sports, Strategy
fields.inferred_semantic_summary: The "Genre" field categorizes each video game in the dataset by its primary gameplay style or theme, such as Action, Sports, or Role-Playing, reflecting common industry classifications used to group and market games.

fields.name: Publisher
fields.sample_values: RED Entertainment, Cygames, Performance Designed Products, Angel Studios, ArtDink, ..., Focus Home Interactive, NCS, MLB.com, Nippon Ichi Software, Microids
fields.inferred_semantic_summary: The "Publisher" field records the name of the company or entity responsible for marketing, distributing, and releasing each video game in the dataset.

fields.name: NA_Sales
fields.inferred_semantic_summary: NA_Sales represents the total sales of a video game in North America, measured in millions of units sold.

fields.name: EU_Sales
fields.inferred_semantic_summary: EU_Sales represents the number of millions of video game units sold in Europe for each game, quantifying European sales performance within the video game industry.

fields.name: JP_Sales
fields.inferred_semantic_summary: JP_Sales represents the total sales of video game units in Japan (likely in millions), quantifying market performance by title within the Japanese video game industry.

fields.name: Other_Sales
fields.inferred_semantic_summary: "Other_Sales" represents the total sales (in millions of units or dollars) of video games in regions outside the main markets (such as North America, Europe, or Japan), capturing additional global sales data.

fields.name: Global_Sales
fields.inferred_semantic_summary: Global_Sales represents the total sales volume of a video game worldwide, measured in millions of units or dollars, indicating overall commercial performance across all markets.

16.description: Data loaded from CSV file: 16.csv (Dataset: Pokemon with stats)

fields.name: id
fields.inferred_semantic_summary: This field represents the unique identifier assigned to each Pokémon species in the official Pokédex, indicating their sequential order in the Pokémon franchise.

fields.name: Name
fields.sample_values: Abomasnow, AbomasnowMega Abomasnow, Abra, Absol, AbsolMega Absol, ..., Zoroark, Zorua, Zubat, Zweilous, Zygarde50% Forme
fields.inferred_semantic_summary: This field records the official names and variant forms (such as Mega evolutions and alternate formes) of individual Pokémon species, serving as unique identifiers within the Pokémon franchise and game data.

fields.name: Type 1
fields.sample_values: Bug, Dark, Dragon, Electric, Fairy, ..., Poison, Psychic, Rock, Steel, Water
fields.inferred_semantic_summary: "Type 1" indicates the primary elemental or thematic category assigned to each Pokémon, reflecting its main abilities and strengths within the Pokémon game universe (e.g., Electric, Water, Steel).

fields.name: Type 2
fields.sample_values: Fairy, Steel, Flying, nan, Dragon, ..., Grass, Water, Dark, Fighting, Ground
fields.inferred_semantic_summary: This field represents the secondary elemental type assigned to each Pokémon, indicating their additional combat attributes in the Pokémon game universe; if null, the Pokémon has only a single type.

fields.name: Total
fields.inferred_semantic_summary: This field represents the sum of all base stats for each Pokémon, indicating its overall strength or capability in the context of Pokémon games.

fields.name: HP
fields.inferred_semantic_summary: HP represents the "Hit Points" or health of each Pokémon, quantifying how much damage it can sustain before fainting in the context of Pokémon battles.

fields.name: Attack
fields.inferred_semantic_summary: The "Attack" field represents the numerical value of a Pokémon's offensive strength, indicating how much damage it can inflict in battles within the Pokémon game or universe.

fields.name: Defense
fields.inferred_semantic_summary: The "Defense" field quantifies a Pokémon's ability to withstand physical attacks, representing its defensive strength within the Pokémon game mechanics.

fields.name: Sp. Atk
fields.inferred_semantic_summary: This field represents the numerical value of a Pokémon's Special Attack stat, indicating its ability to inflict damage with special moves in Pokémon battles.

fields.name: Sp. Def
fields.inferred_semantic_summary: "Sp. Def" represents a Pokémon's Special Defense stat, indicating its ability to resist damage from special attacks, measured as an integer value within the game's battle mechanics.

fields.name: Speed
fields.inferred_semantic_summary: The "Speed" field represents each Pokemon's numerical speed stat, indicating how quickly a Pokemon can act in battle compared to others in the game.

fields.name: Generation
fields.inferred_semantic_summary: This field indicates which release cycle or era a Pokémon was introduced in, corresponding to one of the six official generations of the Pokémon franchise.

fields.name: Legendary
fields.sample_values: False, True
fields.inferred_semantic_summary: This field indicates whether a Pokémon is classified as a "Legendary" Pokémon (True) or not (False) based on official Pokémon franchise definitions.

```
   id                name        years                         genre nationality                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       bio                                       wikipedia  paintings
0   0   Amedeo Modigliani  1884 - 1920                 Expressionism     Italian                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               Amedeo Clemente Modigliani (Italian pronunciation: [ameˈdɛːo modiʎˈʎaːni]; 12 July 1884 – 24 January 1920) was an Italian Jewish painter and sculptor who worked mainly in France. He is known for portraits and nudes in a modern style characterized by elongation of faces, necks, and figures that were not received well during his lifetime but later found acceptance. Modigliani spent his youth in Italy, where he studied the art of antiquity and the Renaissance. In 1906 he moved to Paris, where he came into contact with such artists as Pablo Picasso and Constantin Brâncuși. By 1912 Modigliani was exhibiting highly stylized sculptures with Cubists of the Section d'Or group at the Salon d'Automne.  http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Amedeo_Modigliani        193
1   1  Vasiliy Kandinskiy  1866 - 1944  Expressionism,Abstractionism     Russian                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            Wassily Wassilyevich Kandinsky (Russian: Васи́лий Васи́льевич Канди́нский, tr. Vasíliy Vasílʹevich Kandínskiy) (16 December [O.S. 4 December] 1866 – 13 December 1944) was a Russian painter and art theorist.  http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wassily_Kandinsky         88
2   2        Diego Rivera  1886 - 1957       Social Realism,Muralism     Mexican                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  Diego María de la Concepción Juan Nepomuceno Estanislao de la Rivera y Barrientos Acosta y Rodríguez, known as Diego Rivera (Spanish pronunciation: [ˈdjeɣo riˈβeɾa]; December 8, 1886 – November 24, 1957) was a prominent Mexican painter. His large frescoes helped establish the Mexican mural movement in Mexican art. Between 1922 and 1953, Rivera painted murals in, among other places, Mexico City, Chapingo, Cuernavaca, San Francisco, Detroit, and New York City. In 1931, a retrospective exhibition of his works was held at the Museum of Modern Art in New York. Rivera had a volatile marriage with fellow Mexican artist Frida Kahlo.       http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Diego_Rivera         70
3   3        Claude Monet  1840 - 1926                 Impressionism      French  Oscar-Claude Monet (; French: [klod mɔnɛ]; 14 November 1840 – 5 December 1926) was a French painter, a founder of French Impressionist painting and the most consistent and prolific practitioner of the movement's philosophy of expressing one's perceptions before nature, especially as applied to plein air landscape painting. The term "Impressionism" is derived from the title of his painting Impression, soleil levant (Impression, Sunrise), which was exhibited in 1874 in the first of the independent exhibitions mounted by Monet and his associates as an alternative to the Salon de Paris.Monet's ambition of documenting the French countryside led him to adopt a method of painting the same scene many times in order to capture the changing of light and the passing of the seasons. From 1883, Monet lived in Giverny, where he purchased a house and property and began a vast landscaping project which included lily ponds that would become the subjects of his best-known works. In 1899, he began painting the water lilies, first in vertical views with a Japanese bridge as a central feature and later in the series of large-scale paintings that was to occupy him continuously for the next 20 years of his life.       http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Claude_Monet         73
4   4       Rene Magritte  1898 - 1967      Surrealism,Impressionism     Belgian                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                René François Ghislain Magritte (French: [ʁəne fʁɑ̃swa ɡilɛ̃ maɡʁit]; 21 November 1898 – 15 August 1967) was a Belgian Surrealist artist. He became well known for creating a number of witty and thought-provoking images. Often depicting ordinary objects in an unusual context, his work is known for challenging observers' preconditioned perceptions of reality. His imagery has influenced Pop art, minimalist and conceptual art.      http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/René_Magritte        194
```
